enonce.texte:
Match each word with its correct definition.

WordDefinition
1. AwarenessA. To keep something safe from harm
2. CampaignB. A person's knowledge or perception of a situation
3. ProtectC. To suggest a plan or idea
4. PreserveD. A series of planned activities to achieve a goal
5. ProposeE. To keep something in its original state

questions.texte: Match each word with its correct definition.

enonce.texte:
Rewrite the sentences using the imperative form.

Example: You should reduce waste. → Reduce waste.

  1. You should recycle plastic bottles.
  2. You should save water.
  3. You should not throw trash in the streets.
  4. You should plant trees.
  5. You should protect the environment.

questions.texte: Rewrite the sentences using the imperative form.
questions.reponse: 1. Recycle plastic bottles. 2. Save water. 3. Don't throw trash in the streets. 4. Plant trees. 5. Protect the environment.

enonce.texte:
Read the text and answer the questions.

Save Our Planet!

Our planet is in danger. Climate change, pollution, and deforestation are destroying our environment. We must act now!

Every day, tons of plastic waste end up in our oceans, harming marine life. Factories release toxic smoke into the air, causing respiratory diseases. Trees are cut down for farming, leading to soil erosion and loss of biodiversity.

But there is hope. We can all make a difference. Reduce your use of plastic. Reuse bags and bottles. Recycle paper and cans. Plant trees in your community. Use public transport or walk to reduce air pollution.

Together, we can protect our planet. Join a local environmental group. Participate in clean-up campaigns. Educate others about the importance of conservation.

Remember, the future of our planet is in our hands. Act today for a better tomorrow!
  1. What are the main environmental problems mentioned in the text?
  2. What happens when trees are cut down?
  3. What are three actions the text suggests to protect the environment?
  4. According to the text, why should we reduce plastic use?
  5. What is the main message of the article?

questions.texte: What are the main environmental problems mentioned in the text?
What happens when trees are cut down?
questions.reponse: Trees are cut down leading to soil erosion and loss of biodiversity.

questions.texte: What are three actions the text suggests to protect the environment?
questions.reponse: Reduce plastic use, reuse bags and bottles, recycle paper and cans, plant trees, use public transport or walk. (Any three)

questions.texte: According to the text, why should we reduce plastic use?
questions.reponse: Because plastic waste ends up in oceans and harms marine life.

questions.texte: What is the main message of the article?
questions.reponse: We must act now to protect our planet by adopting sustainable habits and participating in conservation efforts.

enonce.texte:
Read the text from Exercise 3 again. Identify the following persuasive techniques used by the author.

TechniqueExample from the text
1. Use of imperative verbs"Reduce your use of plastic."
2. Appeal to emotion________________________
3. Use of inclusive pronouns________________________
4. Use of rhetorical questions________________________
5. Call to action________________________

questions.texte: Identify the persuasive techniques used in the text.
questions.reponse: 1. Use of imperative verbs: 'Reduce your use of plastic.' / 'Reuse bags and bottles.' / 'Recycle paper and cans.' 2. Appeal to emotion: 'Our planet is in danger.' / 'harming marine life' / 'the future of our planet is in our hands' 3. Use of inclusive pronouns: 'We must act now!' / 'We can all make a difference.' / 'Together, we can...' 4. Use of rhetorical questions: (No explicit rhetorical question in this text) 5. Call to action: 'Join a local environmental group.' / 'Participate in clean-up campaigns.' / 'Act today for a better tomorrow!'

enonce.texte:
You are participating in an environmental campaign at your school. Create a slogan (a short, catchy phrase) for each of the following topics:

  1. Water conservation
  2. Tree planting
  3. Waste reduction
  4. Pollution prevention

Example: "Save water, save life." (for water conservation)

questions.texte: Create a slogan for water conservation.
questions.reponse: Every drop counts! / Save water, save life.

questions.texte: Create a slogan for tree planting.
questions.reponse: Plant a tree, plant hope. / Trees are lungs of the earth.

questions.texte: Create a slogan for waste reduction.
questions.reponse: Reduce, reuse, recycle! / Waste not, want not.

questions.texte: Create a slogan for pollution prevention.
questions.reponse: Keep our air clean! / Stop pollution, start solution.

enonce.texte:
Write an advocacy article (about 200-250 words) for your school magazine on ONE of the following topics:

  • The importance of recycling in your community
  • Deforestation and its consequences
  • The benefits of using renewable energy
  • How to keep your school environment clean

Your article should include:

  1. A catchy title
  2. An introduction presenting the problem
  3. The causes and consequences of the problem
  4. Solutions and actions to address the problem
  5. A call to action

Use persuasive techniques: imperative verbs, inclusive pronouns, appeals to emotion, and a strong conclusion.

questions.texte: Write an advocacy article for your school magazine.
questions.reponse: Save Our School, Save Our Planet! Have you ever looked around our school and seen plastic bottles, paper, and food waste lying everywhere? Our school is beautiful, but pollution is slowly destroying it. We must act now! Every day, we produce tons of waste. Plastic bottles end up in the streets, paper is thrown in the trash, and food waste attracts insects and causes bad odors. This is not only ugly but also dangerous for our health. It can cause diseases like malaria and cholera. But we can change this! We can recycle our waste. Put plastic bottles in recycling bins. Use both sides of your paper. Compost food waste in the school garden. We can also organize clean-up campaigns every month. Let's invite everyone to participate! Together, we can make our school clean and green. Join the Environmental Club! Participate in our recycling program. Educate your friends about the importance of keeping our environment clean. Remember, our school is our home. Let's protect it today for a better tomorrow!
